title: pavonia pulidoae
intro: Pavonia pulidoae, also known as the Mexican Rosemallow, is a species of flowering plant in the hibiscus family (Malvaceae) that is native to Mexico and Central America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Pavonia pulidoae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to harness its medicinal properties.
